name: Lanterne
classification_notes: The letterforms shown in the specimen, including the italics and the contrast between thick and thin strokes, point to a transitional serif. The name and lighthouse-themed specimen copy suggest a refined, classical-leaning design.
summary: A 12-style transitional serif by Abel Martins, designed at Family Type in 2024 with broad Latin language coverage and an extensive OpenType feature set.
description: Released by Family Type in 2024, Lanterne is a transitional serif in 12 styles that spans Regular and Bold weights with matching italics. The family includes small caps, oldstyle figures, discretionary ligatures, fractions and two stylistic sets, making it well-suited to editorial and text work that demands typographic depth. Language support runs across several dozen Latin-script languages, with additional coverage of Greek and Thai.
foundry_blurb: Family Type present Lanterne as a typeface designed by Abel Martins, released in 2024 across 12 styles in OTF, TTF and WOFF2 formats. The family ships with a broad range of OpenType features including small capitals, oldstyle figures, tabular figures, fractions, superscripts, subscripts and two stylistic sets, with language support covering over 80 Latin-script languages alongside Greek and Thai.

name: Modern Era
classification_notes: Modern Era exhibits clean geometric proportions with neutral stroke contrast, placing it firmly in the geometric sans-serif category. The monospaced variant and stylistic alternates confirm a contemporary, functional orientation.
summary: A geometric sans-serif by Briton Smith in 12 styles plus four monospaced cuts, updated in 2026 with revised italics.
description: Designed by Briton Smith and first released in 2017, Modern Era is a clean geometric sans available in 12 proportional styles and a four-style monospaced companion family. Version 2.0.0, issued in 2026, brings revised italics and a broad language support list covering Latin Extended, Greek and Coptic scripts across well over 100 languages. OpenType features include six stylistic sets, fractions, ordinals, tabular figures and a slashed zero, giving the family enough flexibility for editorial, interface and branding work.
foundry_blurb: Family Type describe Modern Era as a 16-style geometric sans-serif family by Briton Smith, comprising 12 proportional styles and 4 monospaced cuts. The 2026 version 2.0.0 update introduces revised italics. The family ships in OTF, TTF and WOFF2 formats with extensive language support and a full suite of OpenType features including stylistic alternates, fractions and tabular figures.

name: Athletics
classification_notes: Athletics presents clean, constructed letterforms with geometric proportions and open apertures, consistent with the geometric sans tradition. The specimen and glyph samples suggest a rational, structured design without humanist warmth.
summary: A geometric sans designed by Briton Smith in 2018, offered in 14 styles with extensive Latin language support and a suite of OpenType features.
description: Designed by Briton Smith and released through Family Type in 2018, Athletics is a geometric sans available in 14 styles across multiple weights. The character set covers an unusually wide range of Latin-based languages alongside Greek, with OpenType features including stylistic sets, fractions, superscripts, subscripts, and tabular figures. Its clean construction and measured proportions suit display use, editorial work, and identity applications where a structured, neutral sans is needed.
foundry_blurb: Family Type present Athletics as a 14-style geometric sans by Briton Smith, released in 2018. The family ships in OTF, TTF, and WOFF2 formats and carries broad language support across more than 100 Latin-based languages, with a full suite of OpenType features including five stylistic sets, case-sensitive forms, fractions, ordinals, and a slashed zero.

name: Aime
classification_notes: The specimen and glyph set suggest a refined serif with classical proportions and a range of OpenType features including small caps and oldstyle figures, consistent with transitional serif conventions. The structured weight range and formal specimen copy reinforce this classification.
summary: A 12-style serif family by Briton Smith with a formal character set, extensive OpenType features and broad Latin language support.
description: Released in 2020, this serif family runs across four weights from Light to Extra Bold with matching italics, totalling 12 styles. It includes a thorough OpenType feature set: small caps, oldstyle figures, fractions, superscripts, subscripts and discretionary ligatures. Language coverage spans over 70 Latin-script languages, making it a capable choice for multilingual publishing and brand work.
foundry_blurb: Family Type present Aime as a 12-style serif designed by Briton Smith and released in 2020. Available in OTF, TTF and WOFF2 formats, the family ships with a comprehensive OpenType feature set and supports a wide range of Latin-script languages across its full character set.

name: Universal Sans
classification_notes: Universal Sans presents clean, neutral letterforms with geometric construction and no visible calligraphic influence. The Display and Text optical sizes and its parametric framing reinforce a systematic, geometry-led approach.
summary: A parametric sans-serif system by Briton Smith, split into Display and Text optical sizes with broad Latin language support.
description: Universal Sans is structured as a two-family system, with separate Display and Text cuts each offering 12 styles, designed to maintain precision across contexts from headlines to body copy. Briton Smith frames it as a parametric type system, emphasising measurable, system-oriented thinking in its design logic. Language support spans an unusually wide range of Latin-script languages, covering African, Nordic and Central European writing systems among others.
foundry_blurb: Family Type describe Universal Sans as a parametric type system designed by Briton Smith. It ships in Display and Text families, each with 12 styles, and supports an extensive list of Latin-based languages. A dedicated website at universalsans.com offers the full range of customisation options.

name: Fazer
classification_notes: The letterforms shown — particularly the circular bowls, even stroke weight and clean terminals — point to a geometric sans. Stylistic alternates for characters like 'a', 'g' and 'i' confirm a geometric foundation with humanist-leaning options.
summary: A geometric sans by Abel Martins with a broad Latin character set, several stylistic alternates and Thai script support across 18 styles.
description: Designed by Abel Martins and released in 2025, Fazer spans 18 styles with weights from Regular to Black, each accompanied by italics revised in a 2026 update. The family carries extensive Latin Extended coverage across dozens of languages, and its character set stretches into Greek and Thai. Five stylistic sets offer alternate forms for characters including the single-storey 'a', 'g' and 'i', giving designers room to shift between strictly geometric and softer, more open registers.
foundry_blurb: Family Type describe Fazer as a typeface with 18 styles, available in OTF, TTF and WOFF2 formats. Designed by Abel Martins and first released in 2025, it received a version 2.0.0 update in 2026 introducing revised italics. The family supports an extensive range of Latin-based languages alongside Greek and Thai, and includes OpenType features such as fractions, stylistic sets, tabular figures and a slashed zero.
